name: Alloy
description_short: This is Britannica's encyclopedia entry on metallurgical alloys—metal mixtures with industrial applications—not a financial services company.
description_long: CRITICAL FINDING: The URL provided (https://www.britannica.com/technology/alloy) is Britannica's encyclopedia definition of 'alloy' in metallurgy, not a consumer finance company. The website content is an educational article about metallic substances composed of two or more elements, covering brass, bronze, steel, and fusible metals used in industrial applications. There is no financial product, lending service, loan offering, credit product, or payday alternative service described anywhere on this page. The article discusses historical alloys like bronze and brass, explains alloying elements such as chromium and nickel, and describes industrial uses in coinage and safety sprinklers. This appears to be a data entry error where a metallurgy encyclopedia article was mistakenly assigned a company profile task. Alloy (the financial services company) is a real fintech firm specializing in identity verification and financial crime prevention, but that is not what this URL contains. The current categorization as 'payday-alternatives' is factually incorrect because this resource provides zero consumer finance content.
